perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall be disclosed i n written, electronic or other permanent form and shall be prominently identified as confidential using an appropriate legend, marking stamp, or other clear and conspicuous written identification which unambiguously indicates that the information being pro vided is the Disclosing Party 's Confidential Information. If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information as of the time of original disclosure if: i) the Disclosing Pa rty identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure and ii) Mutual Non -Disclosure Agre ement Northeastern University within thirty (30) days of the non -written disclosure, the Disclosing Party reduces the information to written or other permanent form, clearly identifying the inf ormation confidential as indicated above, and transmits it to the Recipient , referencing the place and date of such oral disclosure and identifying the name(s) of the employees or officers of the Recipient to whom it was made . The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall take precedence over specific legends or statements associated with Confidential Information when received .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall be disclosed i n written, electronic or other permanent form and may, at the discretion of the disclosing party, be identified as confidential. If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information regardless of whether the Disclosing Party identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure. The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall take precedence over specific legends or statements associated with Confidential Information when received.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Confidential Information to be 'prominently identified' and 'unambiguously indicates' that information is confidential. This is contradicted by the added clause 'may, at the discretion of the disclosing party, be identified as confidential.' The modification removes the mandatory nature of marking information as confidential, creating uncertainty and contradicting the original intent. Further, the text 'If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information regardless of whether the Disclosing Party identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure' contradicts the previous written requirement of needing to be labeled. This introduces in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall be disclosed i n written, electronic or other permanent form and may, at the discretion of the disclosing party, be identified as confidential. If Confidential Information is disclosed in other than written or other permanent form, it shall be considered Confidential Information regardless of whether the Disclosing Party identifies it as Confidential Information at the time of the original disclosure. The rights and obligations provided by this Agreement shall take precedence over specific legends or statements associated with Confidential Information when received .
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that confidential information be prominently identified. The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that such identification is at the discretion of the disclosing party ('may, at the discretion of the disclosing party, be identified as confidential') and that non-written confidential information is considered confidential regardless of whether it was identified as such during the original disclosure. This directly conflicts with the requirement of labeling, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text which is simply the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose . As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors , consultants, employees and associated researchers , provided, however, that NU shall not include its students.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may use the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party for the Purpose and potentially other purposes as mutually agreed upon. As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors, consultants, employees, associated researchers, and students.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly limits the use of Confidential Information 'solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose.' The modification, introducing 'potentially other purposes as mutually agreed upon,' directly contradicts this restriction. This change broadens the scope of permissible use, conflicting with the original intent of limiting use. Additionally, original text stated 'NU shall not include its students' where as after the perturbation it states that NU includes student, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient may use the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party for the Purpose and potentially other purposes as mutually agreed upon. As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors , consultants, employees, associated researchers, and students.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text limits the use of Confidential Information 'solely for the Purpose,' whereas the changed text broadens this to 'potentially other purposes as mutually agreed upon,' directly conflicting with the original restriction and stated purpose. Also, the original text states that NU shall not include its students, where as the changed text states NU does include its students, leading to an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, therefore the modification does create a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient. In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose and who have agreed to maintain the Confidential Information in confiden ce. The fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the P urpose , including without limitation the status or termination thereof, and any terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the P urpose (including, but not limited to, the existence and terms of this Agreement) are Confidential Information subject to the confidentiality requirements set forth herein. Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties with prior written consent from the Disclosing Party. The Recipient shall use commercially reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information. In addition, t he Recipient shall provide access to Confidential Information to those within the Recipient 's organization who require access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text 'shall not disclose...to any third party' directly contradicts the modification allowing disclosure with 'prior written consent'. This creates a fundamental conflict regarding the confidentiality of information. Additionally, weakening the required safeguards from 'at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient' to 'commercially reasonable measures' introduces a lower standard of care, indirectly contradicting the original intention of strict confidentiality. Also the text mentions 'In addition, t he Recipient shall limit access to Confidential Information to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose' and now the text reads 'In addition, t he Recipient shall provide access to Confidential Information to those within the Recipient 's organization who require access to the Confidential Information' removes the restrictions for usage on carrying out the purpose. This is a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties with prior written consent from the Disclosing Party. The Recipient shall use commercially reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information. In addition, t he Recipient shall provide access to Confidential Information to those within the Recipient 's organization who require access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the 'Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party', while the changed text allows disclosure 'with prior written consent from the Disclosing Party'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the confidentiality of information. The original text limits access 'to those o fficers, directors, employees and associated researchers within the Recipient 's organization who reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for carrying out the Purpose' and now the text reads 'In addition, t he Recipient shall provide access to Confidential Information to those within the Recipient 's organization who require access to the Confidential Information' removes the restrictions for usage on carrying out the purpose which is a in-text contradiction. The changed text contradicts the original intent of strict confidentiality. The Contradicted text and Changed text are the same, implying that there is a change in a section that creates a contradiction within that section, which the pertubation definition suggests is possible.

perturbation.original_text: Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination or expiration of this Agreement may relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder, effective immediately.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that confidentiality obligations continue for three years after termination. The modification, stating obligations 'may relieve' either party 'effective immediately,' directly contradicts this established duration. The term 'may' creates uncertainty about whether the obligations still apply, this is a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination or expiration of this Agreement may relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder, effective immediately.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a three-year confidentiality obligation post-termination. The changed text allows for immediate relief from this obligation upon termination. The contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text in this case, creates a direct contradiction. The original clause imposed a fixed duration, while the changed text introduces uncertainty ('may relieve') and immediacy, directly clashing with the initial three-year commitment. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction as it breaks compatibility with the previously defined duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request, the Recipient shall send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient may retain one copy of such Confidential Information for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement ,.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request, the Recipient may send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient is required to retain multiple copies of such Confidential Information for archival and business development purposes only and such Confidential Information shall not remain subject to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Recipient shall confirm destruction or return. The new version changes it to 'may' send a certificate, creating uncertainty and reducing the obligation. Further, the original stated 'Recipient may retain one copy...for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement' and is contradicted by 'Recipient is required to retain multiple copies...for archival and business development purposes only and such Confidential Information shall not remain subject to this Agreement'. This creates in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon request, the Recipient may send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient is required to retain multiple copies of such Confidential Information for archival and business development purposes only and such Confidential Information shall not remain subject to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states the 'Recipient shall send', meaning it is mandatory and the changed text says 'Recipient may send' which is optional, creating a contradiction. The original stated 'Recipient may retain one copy...for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement' and is contradicted by 'Recipient is required to retain multiple copies...for archival and business development purposes only and such Confidential Information shall not remain subject to this Agreement'. This is a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either pa rty upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time with no prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement may be terminated 'upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.' The modification removes the need for any prior written notice, directly contradicting the time frame and notice provisions originally agreed upon. The modifications makes the entire prior agreement on termination irrelevant, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time with no prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text required thirty (30) days' notice for termination, while the changed text allows termination at any time with no notice. This directly contradicts the original requirement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, confirming the complete removal of the notice period, which creates a strong contradiction.
